title: Sandbox Banking Integration
description: Schema for a Sandbox Banking (Glyue) integration definition. An integration is a wrapper for composable logic that connects external banking systems through service requests, field mappings, value mappings, and validation rules.

name.description: Human-readable name of the integration (e.g., 'Loan Booking to Fiserv Spectrum').

description.description: Description of what this integration does and which systems it connects.

sourceSystem.description: The system that sends data into this integration.

name.description: Source system name (e.g., nCino, Salesforce CRM, Client Portal).

adapterType.description: Adapter type used to connect to the source system.

targetSystem.description: The system that receives data from this integration.

name.description: Target system name (e.g., Fiserv Signature, Jack Henry Symitar, FIS Modern Banking).

adapterType.description: Adapter type used to connect to the target system.

serviceRequests.description: Ordered list of service request operations that make up this integration.

adapter.description: Adapter used for this service request.

operation.description: Operation being performed (e.g., createLoan, getAccount, postTransaction).

callForEach.description: Whether this service request iterates over a list of inputs.

fieldMappings.description: Field mapping definitions that transform data between source and target formats.

validationRules.description: Validation rules that enforce input/response compliance.

condition.description: Boolean expression that must be true for the integration to proceed.

errorMessage.description: Error message returned if validation fails.

auditEnabled.description: Whether run history audit logging is enabled for this integration.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the integration was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the integration was last modified.
